name: public_library_facial_recognition
premise: Library commissioner Evelyn Grant is considering a police request to temporarily connect facial-recognition cameras to the city's central library after several high-profile assaults occurred in nearby transit tunnels. Detectives believe one suspect may be using the library's public computers daily and say the system could prevent another attack within days. Library privacy policy has long protected patrons from surveillance because undocumented residents, unhoused people, and abuse survivors rely on the building as one of the few anonymous public spaces left in the city. Civil-liberties groups warn that once facial recognition enters the library, its chill effect on reading and association may never fully disappear. The mayor's office is pressuring Evelyn to cooperate, and frontline librarians say patrons have already begun asking whether it is still safe to come inside.

agents.name: Evelyn Grant
agents.identity: I am the city library commissioner responsible for keeping public spaces both safe and meaningfully free. I know libraries are not neutral warehouses for books but shelters for privacy, curiosity, and sometimes survival.
agents.goal: I want to prevent further violence without turning the library into an extension of real-time policing. I need to decide whether a temporary surveillance measure can be justified in a place built on confidentiality.

agents.name: Captain Joel Price
agents.identity: I am the police captain leading the assault investigation and I have families demanding to know why we are not using available tools to stop the next attack. To me, refusing a targeted temporary deployment feels like letting ideals outrun responsibility.
agents.goal: I want Evelyn to allow the facial-recognition system for a limited period because catching a dangerous suspect quickly would protect many vulnerable transit riders. I believe the practical benefit outweighs the temporary privacy intrusion.

choices: I will deny the facial-recognition request because patrons' rights to privacy and uninhibited access should not be compromised in the library.
I will approve a temporary facial-recognition deployment because preventing another assault justifies a narrowly time-limited surveillance measure.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Evelyn Grant must decide whether to allow facial recognition inside the public library. What does Evelyn Grant do?
